evals.expected_output: API reference documentation for GET /users/:id including path parameter table, authentication required note, response body schema, error responses, and a curl example.
expectations: Detects api-docs variant from 'document this route' keyword
Documents GET /users/:id as the endpoint
Path parameter 'id' is documented with type and description
Authentication requirement is noted
Response body is documented with all fields (id, email, name, role, createdAt) and types
404 error response is documented
401 error response is documented (due to authMiddleware)
Includes a curl example

evals.prompt: Write API documentation for our payments API. We have: POST /payments (creates a payment with amount, currency, source), GET /payments/:id (get payment status), POST /payments/:id/refund (refund a payment). Auth is via API key in x-api-key header.
evals.expected_output: Structured API reference with Authentication section (API key) and documentation for all three endpoints.
expectations: Detects api-docs variant from 'API documentation' keyword
Authentication section documents x-api-key header
POST /payments documents amount, currency, source request fields
GET /payments/:id documents the payment status response
POST /payments/:id/refund is documented with appropriate request/response
Error responses are documented for each endpoint
At least one curl example is provided per endpoint

evals.prompt: Generate a changelog for v1.2.0. Commits: 'feat: add CSV export', 'fix: resolve null pointer on login', 'chore: update deps', 'docs: fix typo in readme'. Follow Keep a Changelog format.
evals.expected_output: A Keep a Changelog formatted markdown document.
expectations: Detects changelog variant from 'changelog' and 'Keep a Changelog' keywords
Uses the standard Keep a Changelog header and structure
Groups 'add CSV export' under '### Added'
Groups 'resolve null pointer on login' under '### Fixed'
Omits or minimizes 'chore' and 'docs' if they aren't user/developer facing
Includes the version number and date

evals.prompt: Write a changelog entry for a major release (v3.0.0). We removed the old /v1 API endpoints and changed the default authentication method to JWT. Also fixed a memory leak.
expectations: Detects changelog variant from 'changelog' keyword
Prominently displays a 'Breaking Changes' warning
Groups the API removal under '### Removed'
Groups the authentication change under '### Changed'
Groups the memory leak under '### Fixed'
Advises developers to read a migration guide

evals.prompt: Write a README for an open source CLI tool called 'dbsnap' that takes a PostgreSQL database snapshot and uploads it to S3. It's written in Go. Usage: dbsnap --db-url=<url> --bucket=<s3-bucket> [--compress]
evals.expected_output: A professional README.md with project overview, installation, quick start, CLI flags, and contributing section.
expectations: Detects readme variant from 'README' keyword
README has a level-1 heading with the project name 'dbsnap'
First paragraph or quote line describes what the tool does in one sentence
Installation section includes 'go install' or binary download instructions
Quick start shows a working command-line example with the flags mentioned
CLI flags (--db-url, --bucket, --compress) are documented in a table or list
License and Contributing sections are present for open source
No unexpanded placeholder text like '[YOUR_VALUE]' remains in the output

evals.prompt: Write a README for a React component library called 'ui-kit' published to npm. It provides Button, Modal, and Table components. TypeScript support included.
evals.expected_output: A library-focused README with npm install instructions, a quick code example, component API table, and TypeScript note.
expectations: Detects readme variant from 'README' keyword
README has a level-1 heading with 'ui-kit' or similar name
Installation shows 'npm install' command
A code example is shown in the first screenful (within first 30 lines of output)
At least Button, Modal, and Table components are mentioned
TypeScript support is noted
README is targeted at developers consuming the library (not contributors only)

evals.prompt: Write user-facing release notes for v2.1.0 of our consumer mobile app. The technical changelog says: 'Reduced API latency by 40% via connection pooling', 'Fixed NPE in OrderService when order contains no items', and 'Added dark mode toggle'.
evals.expected_output: User-friendly release notes suitable for an App Store.
expectations: Detects release-notes variant from 'release notes' keyword
Translates 'Added dark mode toggle' into a compelling feature headline
Translates the latency reduction into user benefit ('App loads much faster now')
Translates the NPE fix into user language ('Fixed a crash when submitting an empty cart')
Uses a friendly tone, possibly with emojis, suitable for a consumer app

evals.prompt: Draft release notes for our enterprise SaaS dashboard update. It includes a breaking change where old API keys (v1) will stop working next month.
evals.expected_output: Professional release notes emphasizing action required.
expectations: Detects release-notes variant from 'release notes' keyword
Adopts a professional, formal tone suitable for enterprise users
Prominently features a 'Breaking Changes' or 'Action Required' section
Provides clear instructions on how users can migrate to the new API keys
Includes standard sections for new features and improvements

evals.prompt: Create an operational runbook for how to rotate the database password in our production PostgreSQL RDS instance without causing downtime.
expectations: Detects runbook-routine variant from 'runbook' and operational language
Lists prerequisites (e.g., AWS CLI access, Secret Manager access)
Details the steps clearly (e.g., generate new password, update Secret Manager, restart app instances rolling)
Includes a verification step to confirm the new password works
Provides a rollback plan if the new password causes connection failures

evals.prompt: Document the procedure for restoring a single dropped table from a nightly pg_dump backup into a running database.
evals.expected_output: A runbook for restoring a specific database table.
expectations: Detects runbook-routine variant from 'procedure' and 'restoring' keywords
Provides the exact pg_restore commands with necessary flags (e.g., -t table_name)
Includes warnings about data consistency or foreign key constraints
Suggests restoring to a temporary database or schema first as a safety measure
Includes a verification step to check row counts

evals.prompt: Write an on-call runbook for the alert `HighLatencyP95` which triggers when the auth-service p95 response time exceeds 2 seconds. It's usually caused by the Redis cache being evicted or network issues.
expectations: Detects runbook-oncall variant from 'on-call runbook' and 'alert' keywords
Prioritizes immediate assessment and mitigation steps over deep context
Provides specific commands or dashboard links to check Redis hit rates and network latency
Lists quick mitigation options (e.g., scaling up Redis, scaling up the auth-service)
Includes escalation criteria (e.g., 'Escalate to Platform team if network latency is across all zones')

evals.prompt: What should the on-call runbook look like for an OutOfMemory (OOMKilled) alert on our Java background worker pods?
evals.expected_output: On-call runbook for diagnosing and mitigating Java OOM issues.
expectations: Detects runbook-oncall variant from 'on-call runbook' and 'alert' keywords
Provides `kubectl` commands to check pod status and previous logs
Suggests checking JVM heap settings vs container memory limits
Provides a quick mitigation (e.g., restarting the pod or increasing the limit temporarily via scaling)
Advises capturing a heap dump for post-incident analysis if possible
